scenarios.title: Recover a partially completed mowing order after equipment failure
scenarios.trigger: A hydraulic-pressure alarm stops undervine mowing after 11.2 of 18.6 acres are complete.
scenarios.goal: Retain trustworthy partial progress, isolate the blocked rows, resume safely, and verify the full block without erasing history.
scenarios.context: Carneros Ridge Block 07 has a mapped weed escape before an irrigation set. The field team is connected, but the manager supervises several simultaneous crews from desktop dispatch.
preconditions: The observation and work order share BLK-007
The operator and foreman are assigned
missing_information: Exact repair completion time is unknown at the moment the blocker is first reported
normal_path: Manager assigns the planned mowing order
exception_path: Foreman stops work after the hydraulic alarm
Foreman records 11.2 completed acres and rows 28–34 as blocked
Manager keeps the work order open rather than marking the block complete
recovery_path: Maintenance replaces the leaking hose and pressure-tests the mower
Manager releases only the remaining rows
Manager verifies 18.6 total acres and immutable exception history
branches: If repair evidence is absent, remain blocked
If actual acreage exceeds block acreage, reject verification and require correction
communications: Foreman reports the blocker to the vineyard manager
Manager releases remaining rows to the foreman and operator
scenarios.device_context: Field report on mobile; cross-crew recovery and verification on responsive desktop
scenarios.connectivity: Connected, with every transition attributable and timestamped
compliance_implications: Actual equipment and labor time must remain distinguishable from planned time
scenarios.completion_condition: WO-001 is verified at 18.6 completed acres, and its blocked and partial history remains inspectable.

expected_transitions.reason: Viticulturist records an attributable field observation
Observation accepted into the work plan
Hydraulic alarm requires a safe stop
